steps.explanation: From the dot plot, set A has mean $10\mu\text{F}$ and range $12\mu\text{F}$. Every capacitor in set B is $14\mu\text{F}$ greater than the corresponding capacitor in set A.
Adding a constant to every data value shifts the mean by that constant: the mean of set B is $10+14=24\mu\text{F}$.
Range is maximum minus minimum. Adding the same constant to every value increases both the maximum and minimum by $14$, so their difference stays the same: the range of set B is $12\mu\text{F}$.

steps.title: Apply the shift to the mean and range
steps.content: Every value in set B is $14$ greater than the corresponding value in set A, so the mean increases by $14$: $10+14=24$. The range does not change because both the maximum and minimum increase by $14$, leaving the difference the same: $(\max+14)-(\min+14)=\max-\min=12$.

steps.explanation:
• A: Mean $10\mu\text{F}$, range $12\mu\text{F}$ — The mean is unchanged from set A, but adding $14$ to every value must shift the mean up by $14$. Eliminated.
  • B: Mean $10\mu\text{F}$, range $26\mu\text{F}$ — Both values are wrong; the mean is unshifted and the range is inflated. Eliminated.
  • C: Mean $24\mu\text{F}$, range $12\mu\text{F}$ — The mean shifts by $14$, since $10+14=24$, and the range is unchanged. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: Mean $24\mu\text{F}$, range $26\mu\text{F}$ — The mean is correct, but the range incorrectly adds the shift: $12+14=26$. Eliminated.
